title: Robata Grilled Mediterranean Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty, resinous, and pillowy Mediterranean dish featuring lamb, rice, and robust spices, perfect for a hangover cure, made keto-friendly and cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine, then finished on the robata grill for a smoky flavor.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat, approximately 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, then add the lamb pieces and toss to coat evenly.
Thread the lamb pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece for even cooking.
Grill the lamb skewers on the robata grill for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the lamb is cooking, heat the chicken broth in the ceramic tagine over medium heat on the stovetop.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the tagine and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Stir in the cauliflower rice and cook until it is heated through and starting to brown.
Once the lamb is cooked, remove it from the skewers and add it to the tagine, stirring to combine with the onion, garlic, and cauliflower rice.
Simmer the mixture in the tagine for an additional 5-7 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the lamb is tender.
